channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Star Wars Unlimited A Lawless Time Spotlight Decks Review - Beauty and the Beasts
item.content:encoded:
Star Wars is famous for its force wielders on both sides, but the franchise is also full of iconic characters who don't use the force and aren't always on the side of the law. Smugglers, criminals, and outlaws fill the galaxy, and the latest expansion for the Star Wars Unlimited CCG, A Lawless Time, focuses on these characters and brings along 2 new spotlight decks featuring iconic characters from the Star Wars universe. In this article, we'll look at both new spotlight decks, and the new rules they both bring to SWU.

We've been covering Star Wars Unlimited since its release, and you can check our our Star Wars Unlimited Guide, our review of the original set, and our recent Booster Box Opening of the new set.

Spotlight decks were introduced to Star Wars Unlimited 3 expansions ago and replaced the 2-Player Starter Sets that came with each new expansion. Spotlight decks are designed to be more competitive than the decks previously in the 2-player starter sets, and include Special Cards, including a full set of 3 of each, along with a booster pack to get you started with collecting the new set. The spotlight decks usually highlight new rules for the set or show off new characters in decks designed for their playstyle.

Jabba

The Jabba the Hutt Crime Boss Spotlight Deck showcases the new Credit rule that A Lawless Time introduces. The deck comes with Jabba as the leader and the Great Pit of Carkoon as the base, which is a special card that is available in this deck, but now may also randomly appear in normal booster packs too. Jabba's 50-card fixed deck includes 3 other special cards (3 copies of each), Salacious Crumb, Malakili, and The Sarlacc of Carkoon.

Jabba's deck is all about building extra resources through Credit tokens. These tokens are paid for with resources as normal, and creates a single-use token that gives you a resource. Paying 1 for a single-use resource might seem strange, but it allows you to ramp up and get bigger units out early. If you bought maximum resources each turn between 1 and 3, on turn 4, you could pay for a 10-cost unit.

The Jabba leader card can pay 1 resource to create a credit token by returning a unit to your hand. Jabba makes use of this by having loads of low-cost units with When Played abilities, so you get the benefit each time you play them. An example of this is the Special Card Salacious Crumb, who costs 1, and if you have Jabba as your leader, comes into play ready, letting you build up credits, and still have them ready to attack (or attack twice in a turn). Another great option is Garindan, who when played, allows you to name a card and discard it from your opponent's hand if it matches.

All of this builds up to getting some big units out with Jabba's ability once he deploys. As an action, Jabba can play an Underworld unit from your hand, and if you use a credit to pay its cost, it gets ambush, allowing you to attack with them immediately. Jabba's 2 big hitters are the Rancor, a 7 cost 7/7 unit that deals 7 damage to a ground unit on attack, for a possible total of 14 damage when it hits the table with Jabba's ability. 

Jabba's other big hitter is the Sarlacc, which your base can search for in your deck (if you thematically discard a unit from your hand), which is an 8-cost, 8/9 unit that lets you put a unit from your discard pile on the bottom of your deck, and deal damage to an enemy ground unit equal to its power. You'll want to have Malakili on the table to make it an Underworld creature so you can capitalise on Jabba's ability to give it ambush.

Jabba's deck does have a few ships, but only 2 with reasonable power, with a few others with low power, so the deck is susceptible to attacks from space, but if you can ramp up quickly and punish your opponent on the ground, they might not have a chance to get out any heavy-hitting ships.

Leia

The Leia Organa Someone Who Loves You Spotlight Deck showcases the new multi-aspect cards that A Lawless Time introduces. Cards usually have 1 primary aspect and then either Heroism or Villainy, but some cards in A Lawless Time have 2 primary aspects and Heroism or Villainy. The deck comes with Leia as the leader and the Daimyo's Palace as the base, and Leia's 50-card fixed deck includes 4 special cards (3 copies of each), Han Solo, R2-D2, Lando Calrissian, and Leia's Disguise.

Leia's deck is all about building up experience tokens on units through a variety of means. Some cards do this themselves, like Han Solo Hibernation Stick, who gains an experience token on attack, building up from a 1/1 unit as he attacks more and more, or the Scarif Lieutenant, who gives a unit an experience token when they're defeated. 

Leia's leader ability lets you give a unit +1/+1 for each different aspect it has, and most in this deck have at least 2, and the deck contains Zeb Orellios, who has 3 aspects, including Aggression, which isn't on your leader or base. You can use the Daimyo's Palace once per game Epic Action to ignore 1 aspect on its card for aspect penalties, and this lets you maximize Zeb's ability, which if you have a Command or Cunning unit on the table, increases the when-played damage from 3 to 5. Once Zeb is on the table, you can boost them to 7/7 with Leia's ability.

Later on in the game, you can use Sabine's Masterpiece, which has 4 different abilities that can all acitvate depending on what aspect your have on the table. Zeb has 2 of these, and the others are spread throughout your deck to make it a great combo attack. To finish the game, Mon Mothma Clinging to Hope has a when played ability that lets you attack with every unit you have, even if they've already attacked this phase. These attacks can't target the enemy base, but they can clear the table for an attack from a unit that you've been building up experience on.

Star Wars Unlimited A Lawless Time Spotlight Decks Final Thoughts

Both of the Star Wars Unlimited A Lawless Time spotlight decks are awesome. With both capturing a different feel of the underworld aspect, good and bad, with Jabba's criminal empire, and Leia's growing resistance, doing what it can to survive and building up for a blow against the Empire. Both showcase the new elements that A Lawless Time introduces, with Credits and Multi-Aspect cards, and I like how both decks focus on building up in different ways. Out of the 2 decks, Jabba's is my favorite, purely for the combo building up to the Rancor and having it hit the table with an absolute blast, and I love the cheap bouncing units, building credits to get to that point.

The copies of the Star Wars Unlimited A Lawless Time Spotlight Decks used to produce this guide were provided by Asmodee UK.

Adam is a Tabletop Specialist for TechRaptor. He started writing for TechRaptor in 2017 and took over as Tabletop Editor in 2019 and has since stood down… More about Adam



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/reviews/star-wars-unlimited-lawless-time-spotlight-decks-review-beauty-and-beasts
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Honkai: Star Rail Version 4.1 Brings a "Lazy" Detective and More Later This Month
item.content:encoded:
HoYoverse has announced that Honkai: Star Rail's next major update is set for release later this month, and that it will bring a brand new playable character (although he technically stepped up to the plate in the last update) and more.

Following on from February's transformative Version 4.0, Honkai: Star Rail Version 4.1, as it's aptly called, will launch across all platforms on March 25th. It will continue the storyline started in the last update, in which detective Ashveil continues to try and unveil a conspiracy surrounding the Elation Feast.

Speaking of Ashveil, he'll become available as a playable character in Version 4.1. He'll serve as a 5-star Lightning character who can stun enemies prior to entering combat, then boost allies' stats and debuff enemies by designating one of his foes as Bait.

 An entirely new game mode, the Wispae War Saga, will be introduced as part of this update cycle, and it'll allow you to lead wispae soldiers to do battle within the Graphia Academy, unlocking new companions and building strategies as you go.

That's in addition to the Star Rail FEST, which is the flagship event of this update. It's got three zones for you to explore, each of which features new combat challenges, opportunities for exploration, and much more.

You can check out a new trailer for Honkai: Star Rail Version 4.1 right here. It's a typically action-packed HoYoverse trailer that should give you a good idea of what to expect when the update lands.

Honkai: Star Rail is just one of the many games HoYoverse currently supports. It joins the likes of fellow gacha RPGs Genshin Impact and Zenless Zone Zero, as well as upcoming projects Honkai: Nexus Anima and Petit Planet. The studio doesn't want for ambition, that's for sure.

For now, though, it's all about Honkai: Star Rail Version 4.1, which officially arrives across all platforms on March 25th. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/honkai-star-rail-version-4-1-release-date
